quiz_questions.question_text: What does the y-axis represent on a position-time graph?

options.text: The velocity of the object
The position of the object
The acceleration rate
The force on the object

quiz_questions.explanation: On a position-time graph, the y-axis shows the position (or distance from a reference point) of the object. The x-axis shows time. Together they show where the object is at every moment.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does the slope of a position-time graph represent?

options.text: The acceleration of the object
The total force on the object
The velocity of the object
The mass of the moving object

quiz_questions.explanation: The slope of a position-time graph equals the change in position divided by the change in time, which is velocity. A steeper slope means a faster velocity.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does a horizontal line on a position-time graph indicate?

options.text: The object is speeding up rapidly
The object is at a constant velocity
The object is at rest and not moving
The object is decelerating slowly

quiz_questions.explanation: A horizontal line on a position-time graph means the position is not changing. If the position stays the same, the object is stationary (at rest) during that time period.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does a straight line sloping upward on a position-time graph show?

options.text: The object is decelerating uniformly
The object is standing completely still
The object is accelerating non-uniformly
The object moves at a constant velocity

quiz_questions.explanation: A straight line with a constant slope on a position-time graph means the position changes at a steady rate. This indicates constant velocity, since the object covers equal distances in equal time intervals.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does a curved line on a position-time graph indicate about the object's motion?

options.text: The object is changing its velocity over time
The object is moving at a constant speed
The object is completely at rest the whole time
The graph has been drawn incorrectly here

quiz_questions.explanation: A curved line on a position-time graph means the slope is changing over time. Since slope represents velocity, a changing slope means the velocity is changing, which means the object is accelerating.

quiz_questions.question_text: On a position-time graph, what does a steeper slope mean compared to a gentler slope?

options.text: The object has a smaller mass overall
The object has a greater acceleration
The object is moving at a faster velocity
The object is completely at rest now

quiz_questions.explanation: On a position-time graph, a steeper slope means the position is changing more quickly per unit of time. Since slope equals velocity, a steeper line means the object is moving faster.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does a straight line sloping downward on a position-time graph mean?

options.text: The object is speeding up in the forward direction
The object is moving back toward the starting point
The object is stationary at a fixed location
The object is increasing in mass over time

quiz_questions.explanation: A downward slope on a position-time graph means the position value is decreasing. The object is moving in the negative direction, which typically means it is heading back toward or past its starting point.

quiz_questions.question_text: What are the typical units on the axes of a position-time graph?

options.text: Newtons on the y-axis and kilograms on x-axis
Meters per second on y-axis and seconds on x-axis
Meters on the y-axis and seconds on the x-axis
Kilograms on the y-axis and meters on x-axis

quiz_questions.explanation: A position-time graph plots position in meters (m) on the y-axis and time in seconds (s) on the x-axis. These are the standard SI units used for measuring position and time.

quiz_questions.question_text: If two objects have position-time graphs with the same slope, what can you conclude?

options.text: They have completely different velocities
They are both accelerating at all times
They are moving at the same velocity
They are both completely at rest now

quiz_questions.explanation: Since slope on a position-time graph represents velocity, two lines with the same slope mean both objects have the same velocity. They may be at different positions, but they are moving equally fast.

quiz_questions.question_text: On a position-time graph, the object's starting position is shown at which point?

options.text: At the highest point on the entire graph line
Where the line crosses the horizontal axis
At the rightmost end of the graph line
Where the line begins at time equals zero

quiz_questions.explanation: The starting position is shown where the line begins, which is at time = 0 on the x-axis. The y-value at this point tells you the object's initial position before any motion occurs.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a position-time graph?

options.text: A graph that plots an object's position against time
A graph that shows the forces acting on an object
A chart comparing an object's mass to its weight
A diagram showing the shape of an object's path

quiz_questions.explanation: A position-time graph (also called a distance-time graph) plots the position of an object on the y-axis against time on the x-axis. It visually shows where an object is located at every moment during its motion.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does the term 'origin' mean on a position-time graph?

options.text: The fastest speed the object reaches during travel
The direction in which the object is currently moving
The total displacement measured from start to finish
The point where both position and time equal zero

quiz_questions.explanation: The origin is the point (0, 0) on the graph where both position and time are zero. It represents the reference starting point from which all positions and times are measured.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'displacement' mean when reading a position-time graph?

options.text: The slope of the line at one specific moment
The change in position between two points in time
The total area underneath the graph line
The curvature of the graph line over time

quiz_questions.explanation: Displacement is the change in an object's position. On a position-time graph, you find it by subtracting the initial position from the final position. It includes direction, so it can be positive or negative.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is meant by 'constant velocity' on a position-time graph?

options.text: The position changes at a steady rate, shown as a straight line
The position stays the same, shown as a flat horizontal line
The position changes faster and faster, shown as a curved line
The position drops to zero, shown as a line hitting the x-axis

quiz_questions.explanation: Constant velocity means the object covers equal distances in equal time intervals. On a position-time graph, this appears as a straight line with a constant slope, since position changes at a steady, unchanging rate.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'reference point' mean on a position-time graph?

options.text: The steepest part of the line on the graph
The maximum speed the object can ever reach
The acceleration value at the start of motion
The fixed location from which position is measured

quiz_questions.explanation: A reference point is a fixed location chosen as the zero position. All positions on the graph are measured relative to this point. Positive values mean the object is on one side, and negative values mean it is on the other.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'positive slope' mean on a position-time graph?

options.text: The object is at rest with zero velocity
The object is moving backward or in reverse
The object is moving in the positive direction
The object is accelerating very rapidly

quiz_questions.explanation: A positive slope on a position-time graph means the position is increasing over time. The object is moving away from the reference point in the positive direction with a positive velocity.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'negative slope' mean on a position-time graph?

options.text: The object is moving in the negative direction
The object is accelerating in a forward direction
The object is at rest and completely stationary
The object is moving at the fastest velocity

quiz_questions.explanation: A negative slope on a position-time graph means the position is decreasing over time. The object is moving back toward or past its reference point, traveling in the negative direction.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is meant by 'time interval' on a position-time graph?

options.text: The total distance the object has traveled so far
The duration between two chosen points in time
The peak velocity reached during the motion
The force needed to move the object forward

quiz_questions.explanation: A time interval is the difference between two time values on the x-axis. It tells you how much time has passed between two moments, which is needed to calculate velocity from the graph.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'y-intercept' mean on a position-time graph?

options.text: The velocity at which the object is currently traveling
The point where the line ends at the rightmost side
The total time it takes for the object to fully stop
The object's initial position when time equals zero

quiz_questions.explanation: The y-intercept is where the graph line crosses the y-axis, which occurs at time = 0. This value tells you the object's starting position before any motion has taken place.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'acceleration' mean when shown on a position-time graph?

options.text: A horizontal line indicating the object is at rest
A straight line with a perfectly constant slope
A curved line showing that velocity is changing over time
A vertical line jumping from one position to another

quiz_questions.explanation: Acceleration on a position-time graph appears as a curve. Since the slope represents velocity, a changing slope (curve) means the velocity is changing, which is the definition of acceleration.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student walks 100 meters in 50 seconds at a steady pace. What would the slope of her position-time graph be?

options.text: 5000 meters per second
2 meters per second
50 meters per second
0.5 meters per second

quiz_questions.explanation: The slope of a position-time graph equals velocity. Slope = change in position / change in time = 100 m / 50 s = 2 m/s. The student walked at a constant 2 meters per second.

quiz_questions.question_text: A dog's position-time graph shows a horizontal line at 15 meters for 10 seconds. What is the dog doing?

options.text: Sitting still at 15 meters from the start
Running at a speed of 15 meters per second
Accelerating from 0 to 15 meters per second
Walking slowly back toward the starting point

quiz_questions.explanation: A horizontal line on a position-time graph means the position is not changing. The dog is staying at the same location, 15 meters from the starting point, for the entire 10 seconds. It is at rest.

quiz_questions.question_text: Two cyclists start from the same point. Cyclist A's position-time line is steeper than Cyclist B's. Who is faster?

options.text: Cyclist B because a gentler slope means faster
Both are traveling at exactly the same speed
Neither is moving because the graph is wrong
Cyclist A because a steeper slope means faster

quiz_questions.explanation: On a position-time graph, a steeper slope means a greater velocity. Cyclist A's steeper line means Cyclist A covers more distance per unit of time, so Cyclist A is traveling faster than Cyclist B.

quiz_questions.question_text: A car's position-time graph shows a straight line going from 0 m to 200 m in 10 seconds. What is the car's velocity?

options.text: 2000 meters per second
10 meters per second
20 meters per second
100 meters per second

quiz_questions.explanation: Velocity equals the slope of a position-time graph. Slope = (200 m - 0 m) / (10 s - 0 s) = 200/10 = 20 m/s. The car travels at a constant 20 meters per second.

quiz_questions.question_text: A jogger's position-time graph curves upward, getting steeper over time. What does this tell you about the jogger?

options.text: The jogger is speeding up over time
The jogger is slowing down over time
The jogger is running at a constant pace
The jogger has stopped and is resting

quiz_questions.explanation: A curve that gets steeper means the slope is increasing. Since slope equals velocity on a position-time graph, an increasing slope means the jogger is accelerating and moving faster over time.

quiz_questions.question_text: A cat walks 20 m away from home, then walks 20 m back. What does its position-time graph look like?

options.text: A horizontal line at 20 meters for the whole trip
A line going up to 20 m then coming back down to 0 m
A straight line going up to 40 meters continuously
A line that stays flat at zero the entire time

quiz_questions.explanation: The cat first moves away from home, so position increases to 20 m (line goes up). Then it returns, so position decreases back to 0 m (line goes down). The graph forms an inverted-V shape.

quiz_questions.question_text: A toy car's position-time graph shows a line from 30 m to 10 m over 4 seconds. What is the toy car's velocity?

options.text: 10 meters per second forward
20 meters per second forward
5 meters per second forward
-5 meters per second (backward)

quiz_questions.explanation: Velocity = (final position - initial position) / time = (10 - 30) / 4 = -20/4 = -5 m/s. The negative sign tells us the toy car is moving backward, toward the reference point.

quiz_questions.question_text: A delivery truck's position-time graph shows three segments: upward slope, horizontal, then upward slope again. What happened?

options.text: It accelerated the entire trip without stopping at all
It went backward, stopped, then went backward again
It moved forward, stopped for a while, then moved forward again
It stayed in one place during the entire trip period

quiz_questions.explanation: The upward slope means the truck was moving forward. The horizontal section means it stopped (position did not change). The second upward slope means it started moving forward again. This is typical of a delivery stop.

quiz_questions.question_text: An ant walks from position 0 m to 3 m in 6 seconds at constant speed. What is the slope of the position-time graph?

options.text: 18 meters per second
0.5 meters per second
3 meters per second
6 meters per second

quiz_questions.explanation: Slope = change in position / change in time = (3 m - 0 m) / (6 s - 0 s) = 3/6 = 0.5 m/s. The ant moves at a steady velocity of 0.5 meters per second.

quiz_questions.question_text: Two friends leave school at the same time. On a position-time graph, Friend A's line is above Friend B's line at all times but they have the same slope. What does this mean?

options.text: Friend A is always farther ahead but both move at the same speed
Friend A is always moving slower than Friend B in every way
Friend B left school before Friend A did on that same day
Both friends are at the exact same location at all times

quiz_questions.explanation: Same slope means same velocity, so both friends move at the same speed. But Friend A's line being higher means Friend A started at a greater position (further ahead). They travel equally fast but Friend A has a head start.
